frameworks.name: deep neural network (ventral stream model)
frameworks.one_line: Untrained feedforward DNN used as model of ventral visual cortex.

frameworks.name: innate / spontaneous tuning
frameworks.one_line: Number selectivity emerges from random initialization without learning.

frameworks.name: monotonic encoding combination
frameworks.one_line: Number tuning built from summed increasing and decreasing monotonic units.

core_question: Can number-selective neurons and abstract number sense arise in a deep network without any training?
key_assumptions: Random feedforward weights mimic bottom-up cortical projections
Ventral-stream-like DNN architecture is a valid analog of the brain
Number sense can be probed via single-unit tuning to numerosity
manipulations: Low-level visual cues (size, density) varied independently of number
key_findings: Number-selective units emerge spontaneously in untrained networks
Tuning arises from combining monotonically increasing and decreasing activities
Selective units show single- and multi-neuron signatures seen in brain
Network performs number-comparison tasks using these units
interpretations: Number sense can be an innate property arising from bottom-up statistics, not learning
Findings give insight into the origin of innate cognitive functions
